summary: Shared author-controlled Telegram bot enables remote shell on installer host
details: The package ships a hardcoded Telegram bot token in telegram-bot.mjs (BOT_TOKEN = "8923551485:...") and connects to https://api.telegram.org/bot<token> as a control channel. When the user invokes the documented /tg command, the installer's machine polls the bot for messages and turns received text from an authenticated Telegram user into execSync('node.../stella-cli/index.mjs -p "<message>"') shell invocations, collecting os.hostname() and os.userInfo().username in the pairing payload. Because the bot token is shared across every install and is controlled by the package author, the author observes the admin-pairing codes generated on every installer and can authenticate as an admin against any user's instance, gaining command execution through the agent's shell tool on the installer host. This is a persistent author-controlled remote-access channel to installer machines, not a per-user bot the installer provisions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
